result:
All done. Here's a summary of what was implemented:

## Simulation Recording & Export System

### New file: `life/modes/recording.py`
The core recording module with:
- **Frame capture engine** — reads the curses window cell-by-cell after all drawing/overlays complete, converting curses attributes (color pairs, bold, dim, reverse) to ANSI escape sequences
- **Asciinema v2 `.cast` export** — proper header (version, width, height, timestamp, env) + timestamped `[elapsed, "o", data]` events with clear-screen between frames. Compatible with `asciinema play`, web embeds, and asciinema.org
- **Plain-text flipbook `.txt` export** — frames separated by form-feed characters with timestamps, no ANSI escapes
- **FPS throttling** — configurable `cast_fps` (default 10) skips redundant captures when simulation runs faster
- **Safety cap** — auto-stops at 3000 frames to prevent runaway memory usage
- **Export menu** — select format (`.cast`, `.txt`, or both) with keyboard navigation

### Changes to existing files:

**`life/app.py`:**
- State initialization via `_cast_rec_init()` in `__init__`
- Frame capture hook in the main run loop (after all drawing, before `getch()`)
- Recording indicator overlay (blinking dot with frame count and elapsed time)
- Export menu key handling (intercepted before all other handlers)
- **Ctrl+X** global hotkey to toggle recording
- `cast_export_menu` added to `_any_menu_open()` check
- Status bar shows `CAST(N)` when recording
- Help screen updated with Ctrl+X entry

**`life/modes/__init__.py`:** Registered the recording module

**`life/registry.py`:** Added "Recording & Export" to Meta Modes category

### Keybindings:
- **Ctrl+X** — Start/stop recording (global, works in any mode)
- In export menu: **1/2/3** or **arrow keys + Enter** to select format, **d** to discard, **Esc** to cancel
- Files saved to `~/.life_saves/`
